name: Pressure Plate
description: A floor trigger activates when enough valid bodies or objects rest inside its tracked volume.
design_notes: Pressure plates are physical state detectors, so enter and exit ordering, object filtering, weight thresholds, reset delays, and stacked objects need deterministic rules. A good plate exposes which objects count and why it has activated or released.
design_purpose: Let players solve puzzles by moving themselves, companions, or objects onto a visible trigger.
Connect spatial positioning, object physics, doors, lifts, and timed routes.
Teach cause and effect through a clear input surface and linked output.
player_fantasy: The player feels they can use weight and placement to hold part of the room in a desired state.

parameters.name: trigger_volume_id
parameters.description: Stable trigger volume identifier used for object tracking and save restoration.

parameters.name: plate_weight_threshold
parameters.description: Minimum total accepted body mass required before the plate enters activated state.

parameters.name: accepted_body_mask
parameters.description: Body classes that contribute to plate activation.

parameters.name: release_delay_ms
parameters.description: Delay after the last valid body exits before linked targets receive release.

parameters.name: tracked_object_ids
parameters.description: Objects currently contributing to the plate state for debugging and save recovery.

edge_cases: Two objects enter and one exits during the same physics tick, changing threshold ordering.
A stacked object rests on another object that rests on the plate and should or should not count.
A carried object is inside the trigger volume but not actually resting on the plate surface.
The plate is saved while depressed and the contributing object is later missing or moved.
common_bugs: Trigger exit is missed when a physics body is destroyed, pooled, or teleported away.
Every overlapping collider on one object contributes mass and activates the plate incorrectly.
Release delay fires after the plate reactivated, closing a door on the player.
Client-side trigger state opens a door while the server still considers the plate inactive.
balancing_notes: Plate surfaces should be large enough for intended objects but not so large that accidental activation is common.
Weighted plates are easier to read when object mass classes are visually distinct.
Timed release delays can reduce frustration when plates control traversal doors.
accessibility_notes: Use plate depression, sound, light, and linked-target indicators so activation is not conveyed only by color.
Avoid requiring extremely precise object placement unless the level supports camera and input assists.

implementation_notes.unity: Track Rigidbody or CharacterController occupants by object_instance_id inside a trigger collider, and recompute mass from unique root bodies. Guard release_delay_ms with a state version so delayed release cannot override a fresh activation.
implementation_notes.godot: Use Area2D or Area3D body enter and exit signals, but reconcile tracked objects each physics tick to recover from deletion or teleport. Store trigger_volume_id, tracked_object_ids, and activated state for save/load.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use overlap events on a trigger component plus server-side validation of body class, mass, and contact state. Replicate plate active state, not raw overlap spam, and debug draw accepted bodies for level authors.
implementation_notes.web: For Phaser, Matter.js, or custom engines, maintain a set of body ids intersecting the trigger and filter to accepted classes. Resolve activation in the fixed simulation step so render frame ordering cannot flicker linked targets.

example_games.title: Portal
example_games.note: Uses weighted buttons and cubes as central puzzle interactions.

example_games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
example_games.note: Uses floor switches and object placement in shrine puzzles.
